idea: Adapted proven 64x128 1p1c wide-N starting kernel to the 2048x16384x2048 target; verified correctness and established the first custom baseline at 255.305 TFLOPS.

Tried a structural ws1p2c 128x128 tile to raise WGMMA issue on the compute-bound baseline, but the added shared-memory footprint and larger CTA reduced net performance to 221.098 TFLOPS, below iter001.
Increased WARP_N from 128 to 160 on the 1p1c base to raise WGMMA work per producer load, but the wider tile reduced net throughput to 175.719 TFLOPS and underperformed the original iter001 baseline.

Tried the stmatrix compiler flag on the 1p1c fp32-accumulator base after SASS comparison, but Choreo codegen fails because stmatrix store currently requires a 16-bit accumulator type and cannot materialize the fp32 output path.
Persistent 1p1c scheduling preserved the 64x128 microkernel but cut throughput to 115.8 TFLOPS, so keeping CTAs resident per SM did not improve this low-occupancy compute-bound shape.

Adding __launch_bounds__(256, 2) to the proven 1p1c CUDA raised throughput to 261.055 TFLOPS, so explicitly steering Hopper toward two resident CTAs per SM helped this low-occupancy compute-bound kernel.

Adding nvcc --use_fast_math on top of the launch_bounds(256,2) best held performance at 260.805 TFLOPS but did not beat the 261.055 TFLOPS baseline, so fast math is neutral-to-slightly negative on this kernel.

Explicitly unrolling the fixed 4-step WGMMA loop on top of launch_bounds(256,2) held performance at 260.853 TFLOPS but still missed the 261.055 TFLOPS best, so manual unrolling is neutral-to-slightly negative here.
Moving warpgroup_arrive out of the 4-step WGMMA micro-loop raised throughput to 262.241 TFLOPS, showing that reducing per-stage synchronization overhead helps this compute-bound 1p1c kernel.

Adding explicit unroll on top of the iter009 arrive_once synchronization cleanup held performance at 262.212 TFLOPS, so the barrier fix helps but extra loop unrolling is neutral-to-slightly negative on this base.

Adding nvcc --use_fast_math on top of the iter009 arrive_once synchronization cleanup nudged throughput up to 262.281 TFLOPS, so math contraction is slightly additive once the redundant warpgroup arrives are removed.

Remapping CTAs through a 4-way block swizzle on top of iter011 collapsed throughput to 232.022 TFLOPS, so this wide-N 32x128 tile grid prefers the original launch order over swizzled block scheduling.

Promoting the two input TMA tensor maps to L2_256B on top of iter011 raised throughput to 263.300 TFLOPS, indicating that a small amount of input tile cache residency helps this wide-N kernel without changing its compute pipeline.
